tool.name: makenucseq
tool.summary: Create deterministic nucleotide sequence records from a bounded random generator

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `makenucseq` creates one or more deterministic nucleotide sequence records from a documented seed and a bounded canonical alphabet. The first EMBOSS-RS release intentionally treats this as governed synthetic sequence generation rather than a broad stochastic simulation surface.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface requires an identifier prefix and an exact residue length. Optional flags control `--count`, `--seed`, `--molecule` (`dna` or `rna`), and a shared description string attached to generated records.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits one or more FASTA sequence records through the shared output path. CLI summaries report the identifier prefix, length, count, seed, chosen molecule kind, and FASTA output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs makenucseq`. Validation currently covers deterministic DNA and RNA generation, identifier-prefix behavior, and explicit molecule handling in the Rust tool and service layers.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The v1 surface supports only canonical DNA or RNA alphabets, exact-length generation, and deterministic seed-driven output. It does not model base frequencies, ambiguity symbols, motifs, or quality-score simulation.

artifacts.label: makenucseq governed case note
artifacts.description: Repository-managed note describing the deterministic RNA generation case used for the governed `makenucseq` surface.

examples.title: Generate two deterministic RNA records
examples.description: Builds two six-residue RNA records from a documented seed and a shared identifier prefix.

expected_outputs.label: Generated RNA records
expected_outputs.description: Two RNA FASTA records named `made_nuc_1` and `made_nuc_2` are emitted in stable order, and the result summary reports the deterministic seed and count.
